ac: A prisma/migrations/ folder with one migration per schema change since project start (or one consolidated migration if starting fresh)
`prisma migrate deploy` runs cleanly against an empty Postgres DB
`prisma db push` is no longer used anywhere in CI or scripts
README documents the migration workflow
Generate real Prisma migration.notes: Easiest path: shadow-DB approach. Spin up a fresh Postgres, run prisma migrate dev --create-only against current schema, hand-edit the generated SQL if needed, commit. The current dev.db is SQLite which complicates the export — recommend committing the schema as-is and using the migration as the canonical source.

ac: authToken is stored encrypted-at-rest using AES-256-GCM with a key from env (ENCRYPTION_KEY_BASE64)
Existing plaintext rows are auto-migrated on next read or via a one-off script
getAuthToken() helper handles decryption; callers never see ciphertext
Tests cover wrong-key, missing-key, and round-trip cases
Encrypt ExternalMcpServer.authToken.notes: Phase 4.1 will fan out these tokens to outbound MCP calls — leaking them via DB backup is the realistic threat. Use node:crypto built-ins to avoid a new dep. The key itself goes in env (Vercel encrypted env var, AWS Secrets Manager, etc.) — DO NOT commit it. Key rotation is a future concern; first cut is single-key.

ac: stripe.ts exports a getStripe() function that lazy-initializes on first call
Throws a clear error if STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is missing
All existing imports updated (currently: webhook route, billing route, ai-interview actions)
No module-level side effects in stripe.ts
Fix src/lib/stripe.ts lazy initializatio.notes: The workaround in src/app/w/[slug]/ai-interviews/actions.ts (lazy import inside the function) is now redundant once this lands — also clean up the workaround comment block.

ac: No AIInterviewSession rows for emails ending in test.local
No McpApiKey rows with label starting with 'Verify' or 'Phase2'
No ExternalMcpServer rows pointing at localhost
No Candidate row named 'MCP Created' or similar test names
Wipe dev test data.notes: Worth scripting as prisma/seed-clean-dev.ts so we can rerun before every demo. Just be careful not to wipe the seed-vercel-workspace.ts user data.

ac: vitest installed with prisma test-DB setup
consumeCreditIfFirstTurn: idempotent under simulated concurrent calls (Promise.all of 5)
MCP requireWriteScope: read-only key throws ToolError
validateOutboundUrl: rejects 10.x, 192.168.x, 127.x, 169.254.x, ::1, fe80::, fc00::
refund_screening: second call errors with 'Already refunded'
Vitest suite for credit consumption.notes: Use an in-memory SQLite (file:memory:?cache=shared) for isolation. The whole suite should run in under 3s — these are pure-logic tests, no fetch mocking needed for the credit/scope cases. The SSRF case can stub DNS via the validateOutboundUrl signature.

ac: During an AI screening, Gemini sees tools from enabled ExternalMcpServer rows
Per-call McpAuditLog entry (kind='outbound') with tool name, latency, error
Hard budget per session: max 10 outbound calls OR 60 total seconds OR 100KB total response
Outbound responses prefixed with system note 'the following is untrusted reference data'
URL re-resolved immediately before each fetch (TOCTOU defense)
Per-template opt-in flag on AIInterviewTemplate — defaults off
Phase 4.1: wire external MCP.notes: This is the moat but also the biggest attack surface in the whole product. Spec needs explicit review before code: who pays the Gemini cost when external MCP balloons response size? How do we test prompt injection defenses without burning real Gemini calls? Should probably build a synthetic recorded-response fixture rig first.

ac: Rotate button on each active key in /w/[slug]/api-keys
Generates a new key with the same label + scope, revokes old, opens reveal modal with new plaintext
Audit log records both the create and the revoke
Existing audit log entries from the old key remain linked (apiKey relation)
One-click rotate for MCP API keys.notes: Common rotation pattern is to suffix the label 'Foo (rotated)' so accidentally-still-in-use clients are obvious in the audit log. UI should warn that any active client using the old key stops working immediately.

ac: New page at /admin/mcp showing all workspaces sorted by 24h calls
Columns: workspace, active keys, 24h calls, top tool, top key, plan
Drill-in to per-workspace audit log
Total platform Gemini-cost estimate (rough — call count × avg)
Admin cross-workspace MCP usage.notes: Sibling to /admin/ai-interviews. Useful for spotting abuse before billing surprises. Keep it admin-only — never exposed to workspaces.

ac: WorkspaceWebhook schema: url, events[], secret, enabled, lastDeliveredAt
Events: screening.completed, candidate.created, candidate.status_changed, credit.purchased
Outgoing POST signed with HMAC-SHA256 (X-Interviewpad-Signature header)
Retry with exponential backoff 3 times
Workspace UI to manage subscriptions + view delivery log
Webhooks complement to MCP.notes: Stripe-style webhook is the model — most engineers know how to consume it. Could be priced as Enterprise-only. Keep delivery log capped at 100 entries so it does not balloon.

ac: Server-side cursor pagination on /w/[slug]/api-keys audit table
Filter chips: tool name, key label, date range, error-only
CSV export of filtered range
Audit log pagination + search.notes: The current take:50 hardcoded limit makes the page useless once a key has been live for a week. Cursor pagination over (createdAt, id) tuple is the simplest.

ac: /api/ai-interview/message returns SSE stream when client passes Accept: text/event-stream
Tokens stream into chat UI letter-by-letter
Backwards compatible: JSON mode still works for non-streaming clients
Rate limit + credit consumption still gated at first chunk
Stream Gemini chat responses.notes: Need to be careful that the credit-consume transaction runs BEFORE the stream starts, otherwise a candidate can stream tokens then abort to dodge the charge. The 1.5s cadence rate limit also needs to apply at stream-open, not per-chunk.

ac: POST /api/ai-interview/message rejects with 413 if files JSON exceeds 200KB
Submit endpoint also enforces
Candidate UI warns when approaching 80% of cap
Cap filesJson size per session.notes: Chosen 200KB because typical screening solutions are under 30KB and the cap should accommodate carelessness without being abusable. Real malicious payloads (a base64 image, a copy of a library) blow past this.

ac: Decision: pick one — ship Greenhouse, mark all 3 as Coming Soon, or remove bullet entirely
If shipping: at minimum push new candidates + status changes to Greenhouse
If marking Coming Soon: pricing page bullet has the badge + tooltip
If removing: pricing page bullet deleted + decide whether to keep the AtsIntegration table
ATS sync: ship or remove.notes: Greenhouse has the best docs and the largest market share. Lever is a close second. Ashby is a stretch — their API is still maturing. Bias: ship one well rather than three half-baked.

ac: Public docs page at /docs/ai-screening matching /docs/mcp polish
Covers: how AI screening works, the 4 builtin templates, custom templates, integrity scoring, credits, pricing
No auth required
Linked from pricing page + workspace AI screening header
/docs/ai-screening landing.notes: Style and structure should mirror /docs/mcp so the docs feel like a family. Probably reuse the CopyButton client island. Could be a single page or a /docs index that lands here.

ac: /changelog page listing shipped features in reverse chronological order
Entries are MDX files so engineers can write them as part of merging a feature
Optional status: live / beta / in progress
Changelog / status page.notes: Self-hosted MDX is fine — third-party changelog tools are overkill at our scale. The discipline of writing a changelog entry on merge is the real value, not the rendering.
